title: Natural colour-safe bleach
subtitle: A sodium percarbonate soak for whitening whites and removing stains without chlorine bleach
excerpt: Sodium percarbonate releases hydrogen peroxide in water, giving the same oxygen-bleaching action as proprietary colour-safe bleach products. It whitens cotton and linen, removes tea, wine, and food stains, and freshens greyish whites. It does not contain chlorine and is safe for most coloured fabrics when used at the correct dilution. Not for silk, wool, or fabrics with a dry-clean-only label.
sourceNotes: Sodium percarbonate (2Na2CO3·3H2O2) as an oxygen bleach: releases hydrogen peroxide and sodium carbonate on contact with water. Active ingredient in commercial colour-safe bleach products (e.g., Vanish Oxi Action). Stain removal efficacy at 40 to 60 degrees C: standard laundry chemistry reference. Safe for most coloured cotton when used at 1 to 2% solution: fabric care industry guidance. Not safe for wool, silk, or delicate protein fibres: sodium carbonate released is too alkaline.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Mix fresh for each soak. Sodium percarbonate decomposes rapidly in solution and cannot be stored once dissolved.
recipe.temperatureNote: Water at 40 to 60 degrees C activates the sodium percarbonate effectively. Below 30 degrees C the oxygen release is slow.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use the solution immediately. Sodium percarbonate decomposes within hours once dissolved; unused solution should be poured down the drain after use.

glossaryTerms.definition: A bleaching agent that works by releasing active oxygen, which breaks down coloured stain molecules. Unlike chlorine bleach, oxygen bleach does not damage most fabric dyes when used at the recommended dilution.

content.text: Sodium percarbonate is the active ingredient in most proprietary colour-safe bleach products. When dissolved in warm water it releases hydrogen peroxide and sodium carbonate, giving the same

action. It whitens cotton and linen, removes organic stains, and refreshes greyish whites. It does not contain chlorine and will not strip colour from most dyed fabrics when used at the dilution given here. Do not use on wool, silk, cashmere, or any fabric with a dry-clean-only care label; the alkalinity of the released sodium carbonate damages protein fibres.

items.prepNote: 1 tablespoon; available from home-brewing suppliers or eco-cleaning retailers
at 40 to 60 degrees C; a full basin or bucket

content.text: Dissolve the sodium percarbonate in the warm water. Stir until dissolved, which takes about 30 seconds. The solution will be slightly fizzy as the percarbonate reacts with the warm water.

Submerge the item and leave to soak for 1 hour. For heavily stained items, soak for up to 3 hours. Sodium percarbonate works best when the solution remains warm; an insulated bucket or a covered basin slows cooling. After soaking, launder as normal or rinse thoroughly if hand-washing.

Alternatively, add 1 to 2 tablespoons of sodium percarbonate powder directly to the drum with the laundry and wash at 40 degrees C or above. Do not add it to the detergent drawer, as it may not dissolve before reaching the drum.

Store sodium percarbonate powder in a sealed container away from moisture and heat. Dry powder keeps 12 to 24 months; it absorbs moisture from the air and loses activity gradually after opening. Mix the solution fresh for each use; dissolved solution degrades within hours.
